73586.subject: Data & Insights: Spot HYPE ETF Sees Strong Demand
73586.body:
The Data and Insights newsletter brings the highlights of the week from The Block’s Data Dashboard

( https://www.theblock.co/data/crypto-markets/spot )

Gm data enjoyers. This week, we’re continuing to watch HYPE and ZEC’s price performance as we await SEC guidelines around tokenized stock trading. Our team has recently published a report titled " A Deep Dive into the Future of Onchain Liquidity Routing ( https://www.theblock.co/post/396391/a-deep-dive-into-the-future-of-onchain-liquidity-routing )." We recommend giving it a read!

This week, we’re taking a look at HYPE price performance and TCG trading. We’ll also look at crypto TVL, exploits, and how the Hyperliquid ETFs have fared. So grab a drink, take a seat, and let's dive in.

1: Hyper Highs

( https://www.theblock.co/data/on-chain-metrics/hyperliquid/hyperliquid-open-interest-2 )

Chart via The Block's Data Dashboard

*Key Insights* provided by The Block Research

* Hyperliquid’s native token, HYPE, reached a new all-time high of ~$64 on Sunday, having increased by over 50% in the last two weeks alone.

* HYPE’s fully diluted valuation (FDV) now stands at over $61 billion, making it the 5th largest crypto token by FDV and one of the best performing tokens in 2026.
* The platform’s aggregated open interest (OI) reached $9.6 billion last week, a four-month high near a ceiling that has capped it since the October 10th liquidation event.
* HIP-3 OI has also remained near record highs as it now accounts for ~27% of the platform’s total OI.

* The recent repricing of HYPE was catalyzed by reports that the SEC is leaning toward allowing third-party tokenized stocks without issuer consent.

* The rumored policy maps directly onto HIP-3's design, a permissionless market-deployment primitive where a builder deploys its own perpetual market without core-protocol approval
* This strengthens the reframing of what Hyperliquid is, as it competes less on the perps it lists and more as neutral infrastructure that other venues build on, evolving into a settlement and liquidity layer than an outright destination exchange.
* The cost for a competitor to launch on Hyperliquid rather than against it is therefore lower, which further supports value accrual to HYPE.

* Aside from the obvious bull case, one should not be blindsided to a point where the elephant in the room is not acknowledged.

* Over a quarter of the entire Hyperliquid OI, and the bulk of the OI growth, has depended on one counterparty's continued participation: trade.xyz.
* A regulatory reversal, or a failure in trade.xyz’s own risk management or processing would propagate to roughly a quarter of platform-wide positioning and affect the primary driver of Hyperliquid’s leading narrative.

See the Interactive Chart ( https://www.theblock.co/data/on-chain-metrics/hyperliquid/hyperliquid-open-interest-2 )

2: TCG Frenzy Continues

( https://www.theblock.co/data/nft-non-fungible-tokens/art-collectibles/pokemon-t

73577.subject: How SCOTUS both checks and empowers Donald Trump
73577.body:
The Economist’s week ahead

Автор: The Economist


Автор: The Economist

    
    
    Прочитайте цю статтю на LinkedIn, щоб приєднатися до обговорення
Прочитати на LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/how-scotus-both-checks-empowers-donald-trump-the-economist-chg9e?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_02%3BG%2BnV4UcoQcmz2wx50AYLoQ%3D%3D&midToken=AQFTjFOONAfT2w&midSig=1DroZ5jFm6CIg1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_-null-fz96fz~mpl4rjfs~1l-null-null&eid=fz96fz-mpl4rjfs-1l
  
Enjoying this newsletter? Get it in your inbox every weekend, as well as our daily round-up of The Economist’s best journalism, by signing up for free here. America’s Supreme Court still has some big cases to decide this term. It has held the line on Donald Trump’s most egregious grabs at powers the constitution clearly doesn’t give him. But on some concrete policy areas it has let him—and looks set to go on letting him—move the country sharply to the right. Colombia has three candidates running for president, any one of which has a shot. One is a populist right-winger in the style of El Salvador’s dictator Nayib Bukele; one is a leftist with a soft spot for making peace with former guerrillas; one is an establishment conservative. It is a hugely consequential vote, and if the first two reach the runoff, could be hugely polarising too. What to expect in the week ahead: ▸ Next week will see a host of central-bank meetings. Israel is expected to cut interest rates slightly on Monday; a buoyant shekel has kept war-fuelled inflation at bay. Hungary and Sri Lanka follow on Tuesday, New Zealand on Wednesday, and South Korea and South Africa on Thursday. ▸ Salesforce, a software giant, reports its first-quarter earnings on Wednesday. Investors are sceptical about whether it can survive the artificial-intelligence revolution; its shares are down 30% year-to-date. ▸ The foreign ministers of the Quad, an Indo-Pacific grouping of America, Australia, India and Japan, meet in New Delhi on Tuesday. Marco Rubio, America’s secretary of state, is set to attend. Asia’s energy crisis, triggered by the war in Iran, is likely to top the agenda. Lane Greene, Senior digital editor Editor’s picks How the Supreme Court both checks and empowers Donald Trump Donald Trump's most enduring legacy may be his imprint on the Supreme Court. In his first term he appointed three justices—Neil Gorsuch, Brett Kavanaugh and Amy Coney Barrett—creating a 6-3 conservative majority. The court has since delivered several landmark rulings applauded by the president and his supporters. The constitutional right to abortion is gone; affirmative action in university admissions has been curtailed; and former presidents now enjoy broad immunity for official acts. More recently the court has provisionally blessed Mr Trump’s policies on spending, immigration and the restruc

Fear and Greed is in 

 Neutral
 territory and BTC is hovering
near
$77K
this morning. US markets are closed today for Memorial Day,
keeping volume light.

The week ended with more questions than answers about Bitcoin's
direction. But a few stories cut through the noise. A legal
settlement closed a chapter on FTX. A key regulatory provision
could reshape how crypto yield works entirely. And a security
crisis in one country is raising uncomfortable questions about
what KYC data collection actually costs ordinary holders.

Let's dive in.

Latest News
-----------

Bitcoin Just Ended Its Longest Underperformance Streak Ever

Bitcoin just ended a 142-day stretch of underperformance
against the S&P 500, the longest in its history. Former Credit
Suisse portfolio chief Mark Connors said BTC is now entering an
outperformance phase.

He described BTC as having a consistent pattern of absorbing
early macro losses before recovering ahead of other assets.

What macro conditions does Connors say make bonds vulnerable
right now?

Read More  
( https://link.coinmarketcap.com/f/a/y6_F09TZhwrFfodhiYHATg~~/AAQRxRA~/bGz3I-AOfLd-PRv-kMs8Adjxqxw_xlNNaJGTNK1cb2rOeoHTa3SHExdMz1UuUwfNAwT9NlE4-g-J87bjyzP87EZxUvXtSAyYHFVby0bV3EOwPO1cV2bOA-_YZFEeU3ehTa-tKv2mt1cgZhXvEvhkJ7_odyB6FgRGZTKGsngTlIw~ )
